tabs.tab_content: No, our OrganoidPlates come precultured and loaded with your organoids of interest. Upon reception our plate is ready for assay after a half-day release protocol. This allows you to quickly start testing and not delay your experiments.
Our OrganoidPlate production methods do not natively include extracellular matrix. However, you can test using your own extracellular matrix after the release protocol (collagen-based reccomended).

tabs.tab_title: What type of cells do we offer in our models?
tabs.tab_content:
On our custom options, you can select up to 5 cell types including:

  • Organ-specific/Parenchymal cells (e.g: Adipocyte)
  • Immune cells (e.g: Macrophages, Dendritic, Monocytes)
  • Endothelial cells (e.g: HUVEC, CAEC, AdEC)  
  • Stromal cells (e.g: Fibroblasts, CAF)
  • Stem cells (e.g: ADSC, MSC)

You may aditionally choose your cell type ratios to best fit your purpose.

tabs.tab_title: Availability of other multiwell plate formats.
tabs.tab_content: Our OrganoidPlate is currently only available in a standard 24 multiwell plate format.

tabs.tab_title: How do we  transport our plates?
tabs.tab_content: Our main competitive advantage is being able to ship at room temperature enabling significantly lowered costs and logistical complexity of transport.

tabs.tab_title: How long can I store OrganoidPlates after reception?
tabs.tab_content:
Depending on the type of plate, storage is possible from 3 days (AdipoPlate) to 14 days at room temperature before the release protocol.

We do not recommend freezing the plates to avoid significant and massive cell death.
